title: SSE UTF-8 decode silently corrupts frame data on invalid byte sequences
description: client.rs subscribe_events(): String::from_utf8_lossy(&bytes) replaces any invalid UTF-8 byte sequences with U+FFFD REPLACEMENT CHARACTER before appending to buf. If a server sends malformed UTF-8 (e.g. a truncated multi-byte sequence split across two HTTP chunks), the data is silently corrupted, the JSON parse fails, and the event is returned as SseEvent::Unknown. The caller cannot distinguish a corrupted frame from a keepalive. The correct behavior is to return an error on invalid UTF-8, not silently replace bytes.
